clusters.subject: Arts (enseignements artistiques)
clusters.summary: Your child is building a little repertoire of songs and rhymes they can sing from memory, learning to stay in tune by listening and copying, and to sing with feeling and clear words. Along the way they discover how their voice works, from breathing and posture to the difference between a spoken and a sung voice. Singing together like this builds confidence, listening skills, and a first sense of music.

clusters.subject: Arts (enseignements artistiques)
clusters.summary: Your child is discovering that almost anything can make a sound, and is experimenting with how sounds differ in loudness, pitch, timbre, and length. They invent their own short sound sequences and find playful ways to show music through drawings or body movement. This hands-on exploration nurtures curiosity and creativity, and lays the groundwork for really listening to and understanding music.

clusters.subject: Arts (enseignements artistiques)
clusters.summary: Your child is making art with their hands, discovering how a gesture or a tool changes the mark or shape they create, and trying out drawing, collage, modelling, sculpture, and photography. They handle real materials, notice how they feel and look, and learn to welcome happy accidents as part of the work rather than mistakes. This freedom to experiment helps them express what they see and imagine, and builds confidence in their own ideas.

clusters.subject: Arts (enseignements artistiques)
clusters.domain: La narration et le témoignage par les images
clusters.summary: Your child is learning that pictures can tell a story or show what really happened, and is making their own images to do just that. They combine words and pictures, and transform existing images or objects by cutting, reassembling, and reimagining them into something new. This helps them see images not just as things to look at, but as a language they can use to share ideas and experiences.

clusters.subject: Arts (enseignements artistiques)
clusters.summary: Your child is discovering that people across times and cultures have pictured the world in many different ways, and is using drawing as their own personal way to express and share, not just to copy. They also try out a range of tools, including digital ones, to create images. This opens their eyes to how varied art can be and gives them their own voice as a maker of images.

clusters.subject: Arts (enseignements artistiques)
clusters.domain: Se repérer dans les domaines des arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your child is meeting real artworks and learning to put words to the emotions and interest they stir up, while listening to and respecting how classmates react differently. They begin connecting the everyday images around them to the wider world of art, and get a first taste of works from France and beyond. This early exposure builds cultural curiosity and the confidence to share an opinion about art.

clusters.subject: Arts (enseignements artistiques)
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to talk about music, putting their feelings and preferences into words while listening to and respecting others' tastes. They also pick up the habits of making music together: listening carefully, playing in time, and following the rules of a group. These skills grow both their musical sensitivity and their ability to cooperate and share.

clusters.subject: Arts (enseignements artistiques)
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to listen closely to sounds and pieces of music, describing and comparing them to spot what is similar, different, or contrasting. They discover some great works from varied styles and eras, and start to notice simple patterns, like a melody or rhythm that comes back. Picking up a basic vocabulary for pitch, tempo, and intensity gives them the tools to really hear music, not just have it play in the background.

clusters.subject: Arts du cirque
clusters.summary: By the end of upper secondary, your child has chosen one or two circus disciplines to specialize in and can perform them with real technique and a style of their own. They can put into words what circus means to them, commit to a creative project and defend the choices behind it, and perform safely by knowing and respecting the limits of their own body.

clusters.subject: Arts du cirque
clusters.summary: In this first year of the specialty, your child learns to put themselves on stage in front of others, exploring how they relate to themselves, to their partners, and to the space around them. They build a personal portfolio showing the skills they have gained and take part in creating and performing a group circus piece, all while wrestling with the question of how to find their own place within a collective.

clusters.subject: Arts du cirque
clusters.summary: Your child starts hands-on circus and gets a feel for its three big families of disciplines: acrobatics, juggling and object handling, and balancing. Alongside the physical work, they take their first steps as a performer in front of an audience and begin to see how circus connects to other art forms and how a circus show is actually built.

clusters.subject: Arts du cirque
clusters.summary: Your child deepens both their practice and their eye as a spectator, going to see live shows to feel emotions and sharpen their own taste. They learn to analyze a work, artist, or movement thoughtfully, to place it in its context, and to carry a creation through every stage of the process, from first idea to finished piece.

clusters.subject: Arts du cirque
clusters.summary: This introductory year lets your child try circus for real while building a broad sense of it as an art form. Living through the full arc of a creation, from early ideas to meeting an audience, also helps them reflect on their own interests and where they might want to head next.

clusters.subject: Arts du cirque
clusters.summary: Your child builds genuine depth in circus, learning its history, its disciplines, and the imaginary worlds it draws on. They practice connecting what they know in theory with what they do with their bodies, and use their growing judgment to help shape their choices about further study and future paths.

clusters.subject: Arts du cirque
clusters.summary: In class, your child learns practical ways to build and structure a creation, and studies the real world of circus, its institutions, professions, and how these skills carry over to other careers. They also work with tools to document and reflect on their own experiences and get to know the set works and themes assigned for the year.

clusters.subject: Arts du cirque
clusters.summary: Your child's progress is judged partly through their own reflection: they review video of their performances, keep a logbook of their circus experiences, and present it out loud. They also show what they have learned by writing and speaking critically about circus and by creating and performing a piece of their own, guided by the question of why they step into the ring.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager steps back to look at the big questions that run through all art: how it feeds off science and technology, how it travels and mixes across cultures, and how artists respond to history and politics. At this age they are old enough to see art not just as objects to make but as a way of thinking about the world and society.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to slow down and really pay attention to how art feels, while broadening the range of works and cultures they know. They start asking why art gets made the way it does in a given time and place, growing genuine curiosity and a more thoughtful eye rather than just liking or disliking what they see.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: Exposer l'œuvre, la démarche, la pratique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns that where and how a piece is shown changes what it means, so they plan for the audience from the very start. They practise explaining and defending their creative choices, listening to reactions even when people disagree, and even organising a real exhibition of their own or their classmates' work.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: Exposer l'œuvre, la démarche, la pratique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to talk about their own work and their classmates' with confidence, presenting their ideas clearly out loud and in writing while staying open to differing opinions. They also design how a piece is shown to the public and can plan a full exhibition, understanding that presentation is part of the art itself.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to make art with their hands, choosing and combining gestures, tools and materials to get the effects they want across drawing, collage, modelling, sculpture and photography. They begin to notice ready-made clichés and to move past them, finding their own way of picturing the world around them or their imagination.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your child works through artistic problems by actually making things rather than just hearing about them, choosing the visual means that best serve what they want to express. They try out a wide range of practices, including digital tools, and learn that how a piece is displayed shapes how people receive it.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager takes real ownership of their practice, mastering varied materials and techniques and matching them to their intentions across two and three dimensions. They learn to solve problems as they arise, to welcome happy accidents that redirect a piece, and to gather and fact-check the images and sources that feed a project.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager commits to a personal artistic approach, proposing their own answers in 2D and 3D and being able to name the choices behind them. They handle setbacks and chance discoveries as part of making, use digital tools freely, and understand how the material, the gesture and the tool each shape the final result.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: L'idée, la réalisation et le travail de l'œuvre
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how an idea becomes a finished work, moving from intention to project through sketches, models, photomontages and notes. They see how a project shifts as it is actually made and learn to keep a record of that journey, building a memory of their own practice that helps it grow.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your child explores how a work lives in space and time and how the viewer's own position and movement become part of the experience. They see how the artist's body and gestures leave visible traces, and how mixing art with digital tools reshapes what we even mean by artwork, artist and audience.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La création à plusieurs plutôt que seul
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns what it means to make art with others, sharing tasks and skills from the first idea to the finished piece. They think about the difference between collaborating and truly co-creating, and about the tricky questions collective work raises around who the author really is and who owns the result.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how pictures can tell stories, building narrative images through sequences, installations and clever visual devices. They play with time and movement, weave words and images together, and use rhetorical tricks like metaphor and exaggeration to give their storytelling more punch.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager digs into how figurative images carry a story across space, across different supports, and by combining picture, writing and voice. They study how images convey time and movement through sequences and simultaneity, deepening their grasp of visual storytelling as a real language.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La matière, les matériaux et la matérialité de l'œuvre
clusters.summary: Your teenager explores how the physical stuff of art carries meaning, from the properties of different materials to sculpture techniques like modelling, carving and assembly. They learn to treat everyday objects and spaces as raw material to transform or repurpose, and to see how their own body, tools and gestures shape what they make.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La matière, les matériaux et la matérialité de l'œuvre
clusters.summary: Your teenager broadens their sense of what can count as artistic material, from natural and artificial matter to everyday reality diverted into art. They pay close attention to how a material's qualities become a source of expression, and how gestures, rituals and events can themselves become artworks.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La matérialité de l'œuvre ; l'objet et l'œuvre
clusters.summary: Your child discovers that materials carry meaning, that colour is a physical substance, and that digital media count as real artistic material too. They learn how artists repurpose ordinary objects to give them new meaning, and how the very physical stuff of a work gives it its power.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La matérialité de la production plastique et la sensibilité aux constituants de l'œuvre
clusters.summary: Your child discovers that a colour looks different depending on the paint and the way it is applied, and that a material's texture and behaviour change what they can make with it. They begin to sense that the physical matter of a piece shapes the feelings it stirs, and that an object or image can itself become a material for art.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La monstration et la diffusion de l'œuvre, les lieux, les espaces, les contextes
clusters.summary: Your teenager examines how the setting where a work is shown, its place, situation and audience, shapes the way it is seen. They discover art displayed well beyond museums, in outdoor or temporary settings, and how the person who designs an exhibition can become a kind of author too.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager studies everything that frames how a work is presented, from the frame and plinth to its placement and scale within a space. They look at how a piece made for a specific place works, and how artists and curators use various strategies to draw the viewer in.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La présentation et la réception de l'œuvre
clusters.summary: Your teenager decides how a work occupies its space, whether on a wall, on the floor, projected or online, and thinks about what the viewer feels standing in front of it. They also explore how text and staging around a piece guide how people read it, and the new questions raised by showing art digitally.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La représentation ; images, réalité et fiction
clusters.summary: Your child explores how images relate to reality, and why an image that departs from what it depicts can be more expressive, not less. They learn how space is built on a flat surface, how pictures tell stories through motion and rhythm, and how to tell artistic images apart from everyday visual communication.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La représentation plastique et les dispositifs de présentation
clusters.summary: Your child learns to tell different kinds of images apart and to see that a picture need not copy reality exactly, since the gap between subject and image is expressive in itself. They organise images and shapes to tell a story, and discover how the way a work is displayed changes how a viewer sees it.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La représentation, ses langages, moyens plastiques et enjeux artistiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager explores drawing as a rich art form in its own right and how it connects with painting, sculpture, photography and digital work. They study portraiture, fiction, resemblance and the expressive gap with reality, learning to represent themselves, others and invented worlds from their own point of view.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: La représentation, ses langages, moyens plastiques et enjeux artistiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager deepens their understanding of drawing across its many traditional, contemporary and digital forms, and of how it captures the body and space. They think about how representation relates to reality through resemblance and the meaningful gap from it, and how machines and new tools open fresh possibilities for making marks.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: Les fabrications et la relation entre l'objet et l'espace
clusters.summary: Your child learns to build and work in three dimensions, modelling, assembling and constructing, while grasping ideas like full and empty, contour and volume. They invent and repurpose objects for storytelling or poetic effect, and learn to combine mixed materials so that different elements hold together as one coherent piece.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your child learns to carry an art project from idea to finished piece, working alone or in a group and seeing it through to the end. They plan the stages, anticipate difficulties, and compare what they meant to make with what they actually made, showing growing independence and a critical eye.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: Mettre en œuvre un projet artistique
clusters.summary: Your child learns to plan an art project, working out which tools and skills it needs and adjusting it to the materials and the effect they want on the viewer. They find their way through the steps of making, anticipate snags, and take on their fair share when creating alongside others.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: Mettre en œuvre un projet artistique individuel ou collectif
clusters.summary: Your teenager conceives, produces and shows a real artistic project, alone or with others, and carries it through to completion. They plan its stages, anticipate difficulties, and keep checking whether the finished work holds up against their original intention, working with genuine independence and critical thinking.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager gets to know the main families of art practice: flat work like drawing and painting, three-dimensional work like sculpture and architecture, photography and film, and digital art. This gives them a map of the whole field so they can find where their own interests lie.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager consolidates their knowledge of the major art practices, from drawing and painting to sculpture and architecture, photography and film, and digital media. Knowing these fields well gives them a shared vocabulary and a solid footing for their own more ambitious work.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to build a relationship with artworks that is both felt and informed by real knowledge, staying open to many kinds of expression. They look at works from both the maker's and the viewer's side, and learn to place a piece in its historical and cultural context.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to describe and analyse a work precisely, then move on to a personal interpretation they can argue and defend, in writing or out loud. They build knowledge of art across eras and cultures, including key twentieth-century landmarks, and start to recognise the influences hidden in their own work.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: S'exprimer, analyser sa pratique, celle de ses pairs ; établir une relation avec celle des artistes, s'ouvrir à l'altérité
clusters.summary: Your child learns to put their feelings and choices into words, explaining how they got from an idea to a finished piece. Using proper art vocabulary, they talk about their own work, their classmates' work and the artworks they study, which sharpens both their thinking and their eye.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: S'exprimer, analyser sa pratique, celle de ses pairs ; établir une relation avec celle des artistes, s'ouvrir à l'altérité
clusters.summary: Your child learns to look with curiosity at the images all around them and to connect their own work with the artworks they encounter. They explain what they do and feel using the right vocabulary, and practise listening to and accepting opinions that differ from their own, even contradictory ones.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: Se repérer dans les domaines liés aux arts plastiques, être sensible aux questions de l'art
clusters.summary: Your child gets to know works from many periods and places, from national and world heritage, and learns to grasp what they mean and why they matter. They practise proposing and defending an interpretation, joining the debate a work sparks, and placing a piece in its time and culture.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.domain: Un questionnement artistique transversal : se penser et se situer comme artiste
clusters.summary: Your teenager starts to ask who they are as an artist: a maker, a technician, or an inventor of new ways of working. They weigh whether to follow a tradition or break from it to assert their own voice, and think through what it means to respond to a commission for someone else.

clusters.subject: Arts plastiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager works through real case studies that raise big questions about what a work of art actually is: a physical object, an installation, or just an idea. They weigh whether a piece should be finished or left open, and whether it should stay unique or be reproduced, learning to take a position and justify it.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the body turns food into the energy that powers movement, and how bones, joints and muscles work together to make us walk, run and stay upright. They see why regular exercise protects health while a sedentary life harms it, and why practices like doping or badly managed training can injure the body, so they can take real responsibility for their own well-being.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering what food is really made of, how the body digests it and turns it into the nutrients that fuel and rebuild us, and how blood sugar is kept in balance by the body's own controls. They learn to read food labels and judge dietary claims critically, and they understand how eating habits, and even our gut bacteria, affect health and conditions like diabetes and obesity, while food choices ripple out to the environment.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: Altération du génome et processus de réparation
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that our DNA occasionally changes, whether by chance during copying or because of outside agents like UV light, and that the body has repair systems that fix most of these errors. They see why a change matters differently depending on the cell it hits, and how these small variations are ultimately the raw material that makes each individual and each population unique.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: Conséquences et prévention du stress chronique
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning what happens in the body when stress never lets up, and how the constant flood of stress hormones can eventually wear the system down and contribute to illness. They explore why people react to pressure so differently, and compare ways of coping, from medication to simple habits like physical activity, so they understand how to protect their own mental and physical health.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the instructions in our genes are read to build the proteins that shape who we are, and why the same genes can lead to different outcomes depending on the environment. They study how traits pass through families, follow a genetic disease across a family tree, and meet modern tools like GMOs and gene editing, weighing their promises and their risks for health and society.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the body defends itself against germs through a set of organs, cells and molecules that work together, and how this understanding explains why vaccines protect us. They also see how everyday surroundings can trigger allergies and how our gut bacteria help keep the immune system working properly.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning why living things are found where they are: a series of natural filters, from how far a species can travel to the conditions it can tolerate and the neighbours it must live alongside, shapes each community. They discover how to count and survey populations in the field, and how human activity and climate change are reshaping these communities and eroding the variety of life that evolution has produced.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the diversity of life keeps changing over time, driven by mutations, natural selection favouring the best-suited individuals, and pure chance. They see how species are related through shared ancestry, how reproductive isolation can give rise to entirely new species, and how human activity is now leaving its own mark on the living world's genetic wealth.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: La biodiversité et le fonctionnement des écosystèmes
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how an ecosystem works as a web of interactions between living things and their surroundings, and why its rich variety of species is what keeps matter cycling and the whole system healthy. They explore the real benefits nature provides us, the damage done by pollution and invasive species, and how thoughtful choices like farming with nature and protecting wildlife corridors can help preserve it.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: La dynamique de la biodiversité, stabilité et résilience des écosystèmes
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that ecosystems are never truly still: they change through stages over time and can be knocked off balance by disturbances, whether natural or caused by people. They discover why some ecosystems resist or bounce back better than others, how certain key species hold everything together, and how humans can actively help damaged nature recover.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: La variation des effectifs des populations
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning why the number of animals or plants in a population rises and falls, driven by births, deaths and movement in and out. They use simple mathematical models and measurements to describe how populations grow and level off, and see how competition, predators, climate and different life strategies all push those numbers up or down.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: Le stress aigu, une adaptation permettant d'anticiper un effort physique
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning what happens in the body in the face of sudden danger: a fast stress response, driven by the nervous system and hormones like adrenaline, that speeds the heart and rushes oxygen to the muscles to prepare for action. They measure how these changes work during effort, and understand why this useful short-term reaction can turn harmful when stress becomes constant.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: Le système immunitaire et le VIH
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning in depth how the immune system protects us, from the body's first physical barriers and fast inflammatory response to the precise, tailored defences that recognise a specific germ and remember it for next time, which is what makes vaccination work. They study how HIV attacks these very defences, and how this knowledge is now being used to fight cancer.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: Le système nerveux et les substances psychoactives
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the nervous system carries information as tiny electrical signals along nerve fibres and passes them from one neuron to the next across connections called synapses. They trace how a reflex or a voluntary movement is organised, and understand how certain drugs and psychoactive substances interfere with these messages in the brain.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning what an allergy really is, an over-the-top reaction of the immune system to something harmless like pollen or dust mites, and how it unfolds in two steps that end in the body releasing histamine. They explore why allergies are becoming more common in our very clean, chemical-filled surroundings, and how contact with a rich variety of microbes, especially in childhood, helps the immune system grow up well-balanced.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how our bodies grow and renew themselves as cells copy their DNA and then divide, passing on an identical set of genetic information to each new cell. They follow the stages of this division under the microscope and see how the careful, faithful copying of DNA keeps our cells consistent over a lifetime.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: Transformation des habitats et accès aux ressources
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how living things get the energy and materials they need, from plants building their own food through photosynthesis to animals feeding through predation, parasitism and partnerships shaped by evolution. They see how every species fits a particular ecological niche and budgets its energy between growth, survival and reproduction, and how these ideas apply to real challenges like farming with nature and how pollutants build up along the food chain.

clusters.subject: Biologie-écologie
clusters.domain: Évolution du génome bactérien et antibiorésistance
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning why antibiotics are gradually losing their power: random mutations make some bacteria resistant, and each dose of antibiotic then clears the way for those survivors to multiply and spread. They design experiments to test which antibiotics still work, and understand how overuse across medicine, farming and the environment fuels dangerous multi-resistant strains, a genuine public-health concern.

clusters.subject: CIT / SI (option 2nde)
clusters.domain: Mettre en œuvre une démarche de projet et de créativité
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how real innovation happens: taking a fuzzy idea, generating lots of possible solutions with creativity techniques, and shaping the best one into a genuinely new product that meets a real need. Along the way they learn to work as a team, research the right sources, and keep their designs realistic by respecting rules, budgets, and environmental limits. It is a first taste of how engineers and designers actually invent things, and it builds the habit of turning imagination into workable plans.

clusters.subject: CIT / SI (option 2nde)
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to explain their ideas and technical choices so that other people clearly understand them, whether in writing or out loud. They practice reporting the results of an experiment or piece of research and choosing the right tools, from sketches to slides, to present a project to an audience. These communication skills matter far beyond the classroom, giving them the confidence to defend their thinking and share what they have created.

clusters.subject: Cinéma-audiovisuel
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to watch films the way a filmmaker does: reading a single shot or short scene closely, explaining what it means and why it moves them, and backing up their opinion with real film knowledge. They also start making their own short pieces, choosing how to frame and film a shot, justifying those choices by the effect they want on the audience, and presenting the result. It is the moment cinema shifts from something they simply enjoy to something they can both analyse and create thoughtfully and responsibly.

clusters.subject: Cinéma-audiovisuel
clusters.summary: Building on the year before, your teenager now carries a filmmaking project through every stage, from script to final edit, developing a personal style along the way. They learn to pick the right angle for analysing a work, whether technical, historical, or economic, and to place films within the wider history and movements of cinema. The aim is a confident, well-argued critical eye and the ability to defend their own artistic point of view.

clusters.subject: Cinéma-audiovisuel
clusters.summary: This year is organised around a few big questions that give the learning its shape: how films stir emotion, how a character is built on screen, and how the shot works as the basic unit of film language. Your teenager also discovers the many jobs and the economics behind getting a film made and seen, and traces the history of special effects from early cinema to today. These themes turn abstract film theory into concrete, memorable ways of understanding how movies work.

clusters.subject: Cinéma-audiovisuel
clusters.summary: In the final year the questions grow more ambitious: what it means to be a film author, how genres live from production to reception, how cinema balances art and industry, and how new technologies are reshaping what films can be. Your teenager examines the studio system, how films and their themes travel between cultures, and how different audiences receive the same work. These themes give them a mature, critical grasp of cinema as both an art form and a global industry.

clusters.subject: Cinéma-audiovisuel
clusters.domain: Situations d'apprentissage et expériences de l'élève
clusters.summary: Learning here is hands-on and reflective at once. Your teenager keeps a creative journal alongside their project, studies the year's set works in depth, and learns the successive stages of writing a fiction film through building exercises. They also meet and work with real film professionals, getting an honest look at what the industry is actually like. It connects classroom theory to genuine creative practice and the working world of cinema.

clusters.subject: Cinéma-audiovisuel
clusters.summary: When assessed, your teenager shows both craft and judgement: producing a deliberate variation on part of their project and defending the alternative choice, and building a structured argument about films they present in writing and aloud. They also re-edit fragments of a film to see how small changes shift its meaning, and keep an organised dossier tracing how their own project came together. It rewards not just the finished work but the thinking and reflection behind every choice.

clusters.subject: DGEMC
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning who counts as a holder of rights in the eyes of the law: how people (and even companies) gain a legal identity, when someone is allowed to act on their own, and how being free also means being answerable for what you do. They explore the different ways a person can be held responsible, and thorny questions like where an animal fits between being a 'thing' and a being with protections. It helps them see the legal scaffolding beneath everyday life and start reasoning like an informed citizen.

clusters.subject: DGEMC
clusters.domain: 2.3.4 - Bioéthique et liberté de la personne
clusters.summary: Your teenager is exploring how the law tries to protect the human body and dignity when medicine can do more and more: how far doctors may go, and where the limits sit. They wrestle with real ethical debates, such as whether a person can decide the terms of their own death and why the rules around medically assisted reproduction are set the way they are. At this age it teaches them to weigh competing values, form their own reasoned views, and understand the hard choices behind laws that affect real lives.

clusters.subject: Danse
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to watch dance like a thoughtful, curious observer, letting a performance move them first and only then putting words to why it works. They study choreographies from video, photos and notation, research the questions a piece raises, and reflect honestly on their own dancing so they can talk and write about it with real understanding.

clusters.subject: Danse
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to compose their own dance, playing with the raw ingredients of movement such as space, time, energy and the body to express a clear idea. Working closely with partners on a shared piece, they rehearse, give and take feedback, and shape a choreography they can perform and present to others.

clusters.subject: Danse
clusters.summary: Your child is deepening their craft as a choreographer, drawing on different dance styles to build a personal movement vocabulary and running their own creative project through its full cycle, from research to finished piece. They also work on the expressive control of their body, exploring how to interpret and inhabit a role so their dancing carries genuine feeling and personality.

clusters.subject: Danse
clusters.summary: Your child is discovering how dance really works, from telling different styles apart to sensing where their body is in space and in relation to other dancers. Through practice they build simple working knowledge of how the body moves and how various choreographers approach making a dance, widening what their own body can do.

clusters.subject: Danse
clusters.summary: Your child is connecting their dance experience to the wider world of live performance, learning about its works, venues and the many jobs behind a show. They approach dance with both feeling and clear-eyed method, sharpening the critical thinking that helps them shape their own future path and choices.

clusters.subject: Danse
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to meet dance works with an open, curious mind, welcoming the unfamiliar and accepting that different people can read the same piece in very different valid ways. They also learn to place works and artists in their time, understanding the cultural and social contexts that shaped how a dance came to be.

clusters.subject: Danse
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to show and account for their year in dance, both by performing a choreography they have shaped and by explaining their choices in speech and in writing. They keep a personal logbook and a creation journal that track their experiences and thinking, and they practise discussing and debating dance in a clear, reasoned way.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Acquérir et partager les valeurs de la République
clusters.summary: Your child is discovering what it means to belong to the French Republic: its motto and symbols like the flag and the Marseillaise, the shared values of liberty, equality, fraternity and laïcité, and why rules and laws exist. Through class and school rules, the rights of the child, road-safety basics and a first look at voting, they begin to see themselves as part of a community with both rights and responsibilities.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Acquérir et partager les valeurs de la République
clusters.summary: Your child is building a fuller picture of how French and European society is organized, from the town council to the National Assembly, and how laws are made and applied. They study the great declarations of rights, the meaning of laïcité, equality before the law, and the symbols and commemorations that hold the country together, so they can understand and take their place as a young citizen.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to see difference, whether in background, appearance or culture, as something valuable rather than a reason for conflict. By practising empathy, listening to how others feel, and helping and sharing, they start to understand that solidarity and kindness make living together work.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Axe 1 : Fondements et expériences de la démocratie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is exploring where democracy comes from and how it works, from ancient Athens to modern parliaments, elections and the rule of law. They compare democratic and authoritarian regimes, examine how democracies protect themselves and how laïcité shapes French public life, and think about why these foundations matter and how fragile they can be.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Axe 2 : Repenser et faire vivre la démocratie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is looking at how democracy is challenged and renewed today, through issues like disinformation, corruption, environmental responsibility, bioethics and the many ways people get involved in civic life. They learn about the welfare state, human rights, whistleblowers and the role of a free press, and reflect on how citizens can keep democracy alive and make it work better.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your teenager is practising the skills that make a thoughtful citizen: speaking and debating clearly, listening to opposing views, and weighing evidence in the search for truth. They learn to research information rigorously, read and place documents in context, and work well with others on shared projects.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Comprendre les grands principes des sociétés démocratiques
clusters.summary: Your child is learning the core ideas that hold a democracy together: fundamental rights and freedoms, the declarations that protect them, and the special legal status of the child. They also explore why freedom of the press and expression matter, how citizens make their voices heard, and how to seek out reliable information.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Connaître les principes, valeurs et symboles de la citoyenneté française et de la citoyenneté européenne
clusters.summary: Your child is discovering the symbols and values of both French and European citizenship, from the tricolour flag and the Marseillaise to the European flag and Europe Day. They learn about equality, non-discrimination and the principles of laïcité, and see how belonging to France connects with belonging to a wider Europe.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to know and respect themselves, understanding what makes up their identity, including their online identity, and the difference between personal conscience and legal responsibility. This helps them make their own decisions with confidence, including when facing peer pressure, risk-taking or addiction.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to be an active member of a group: finding their place, taking part in discussions and debates, and beginning to build simple arguments. They also start to question stereotypes, look at how a class council makes shared decisions, and check information carefully across different sources.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is deepening their sense of citizenship by learning to cooperate, argue a point of view, and tell verified facts from personal opinions. They think about the common good, responsible choices, and their role in issues like health and the environment, while learning to use the internet critically and respect the rights attached to online content.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning what it means to commit and follow through, being accountable for their behaviour and cooperating well in group work. By taking on real responsibilities in school life, such as being an eco-delegate, they develop a civic, social and ecological awareness.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to recognise and manage their own emotions rather than be ruled by them, and to build healthy self-esteem. At the same time they practise listening with empathy, understanding others' feelings, and accepting that people differ in origin, belief and opinion, all of which strengthens the sense of living together.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is developing the ability to think critically and form their own reasoned judgement. Through rule-governed debate they learn to express their opinion, respect others', and weigh their personal interest against the common good.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is exploring what it means to get involved, from political and humanitarian action to environmental causes, and how commitment goes hand in hand with responsibility. They look at how women's rights have evolved, how France acts internationally, and the responsibilities each of us carries, including as consumers and in the face of major risks.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: L'élection, la représentation citoyenne dans une démocratie
clusters.summary: Your child is learning how citizens are represented in a democracy through elections and the majority principle. They discover different voting systems and think about the role of transparency and public opinion in keeping power accountable.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning how France protects its people, from the organization of security services to the principles behind national defence. They discover the ways citizens can serve their country and reflect on threats to freedom, and the difficult questions of war and peace in the world.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is discovering who runs France, from the local mayor and town council to the President, Prime Minister and government. They also deepen their understanding of the Republic's motto, liberté, égalité, fraternité, and what each of those words really means.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is exploring the difference between personal ethics and professional codes of conduct, using real examples like the choices of a judge or doctor. They learn the words for moral feelings such as guilt or fairness, and understand how a silent witness to bullying or injustice can end up complicit, and why it matters to act.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Le rôle de la loi dans une société
clusters.summary: Your child is learning why laws and rules exist and how they set out everyone's rights and duties. They follow how a law is made in France, how justice works and who its actors are, and discover key rights around work, property and social protection, along with the difference between national and international law.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Le vote, un droit fondamental en démocratie
clusters.summary: Your child is learning why voting is a cornerstone of democracy and how the right to vote was won and widened over time. They come to understand citizenship as a set of rights and duties, and why taking part in elections, from local ballots to referendums, matters.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is growing more independent, learning to make choices and take initiative without fear of getting things wrong, and to explain their decisions. At the same time they learn to care for shared belongings, recognise dangers and act safely, and respect the different adults at school and the roles they play.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.domain: Reconnaître les grandes caractéristiques d'un État démocratique
clusters.summary: Your child is learning what makes a state democratic, from the role of the Constitution and the institutions of the Fifth Republic to pluralism, majority rule and the rule of law. They explore how the French state is organized, what a nation is, and how the Republic's values can sometimes pull against one another in real situations.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning that every person is equal in dignity and that their own freedom stops where another's begins. They look closely at bullying and cyberbullying, learn to tell inequality from discrimination and recognise its forms, and understand how laïcité protects everyone's freedom of belief.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning the everyday habits of respect: naming and sharing their emotions, listening to others' points of view, adapting their language to the situation, and keeping the promises they make. They also learn to care for their body and surroundings and to help someone in need, laying the groundwork for kind and considerate behaviour.

clusters.subject: EMC
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to treat others with respect and to recognise and reject prejudice, discrimination and harassment in all their forms. Through structured discussion they practise voicing an opinion, listening, and nuancing their view, while also learning road safety, first-aid basics, tolerance, and responsible, safe use of digital tools and social networks.

clusters.subject: EMI
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to be a careful, questioning reader of information rather than believing whatever they come across. They practise checking whether a source is trustworthy, telling real science from things that only sound scientific, and noticing when a news story is stating facts versus sharing an opinion. At an age when they are online constantly, this helps them slow down and think before they trust or share.

clusters.subject: EMI
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to create and share content responsibly, not just consume it. They work on projects together using digital tools, turn scattered facts into something organised and useful, and learn to credit their sources instead of copying, all while keeping their audience in mind. It teaches them that publishing to others comes with real care and honesty.

clusters.subject: EMI
clusters.domain: Utiliser les médias de manière responsable
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to be a thoughtful, safe presence online and to understand the bigger picture of how media shape society. They explore what a digital footprint is, where the line falls between private and public when posting, and the basic rules that keep sharing legal and respectful. They also start to see why honest, trustworthy media matter for democracy, so they can take part responsibly.

clusters.subject: EMI
clusters.domain: Utiliser les médias et les informations de manière autonome
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to research independently and find their way through a sea of information with method rather than guesswork. They get better at using search engines well, choosing the right kind of source for a question, understanding how libraries and websites are organised, and keeping their own documents and findings tidy. These are the study skills that make them a confident, self-reliant learner.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.summary: Your teenager practices across the full range of physical activities: chasing a measurable best (running fast, jumping far, lifting heavy), moving through unpredictable settings like climbing walls or open water, performing a routine for an audience, keeping themselves fit over the long term, and outplaying an opponent in team or combat sports. By living each of these very different challenges firsthand, they build a broad, well-rounded body of movement experience that anchors everything they study in this specialty.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.domain: Apports théoriques — Enjeux de la pratique physique dans le monde contemporain
clusters.summary: Your teenager steps back to see sport as a social phenomenon: where modern sport came from, how it is organized through French federations and clubs, and what the Olympic movement stands for. They also weigh its harder questions, from money and ethics to doping, violence, the environmental cost of activity, and the place of women in sport, so they can think critically about the role sport plays in society today.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.domain: Apports théoriques — Métiers du sport et du corps humain
clusters.summary: Your teenager explores the many careers built around sport and the human body, from teaching and coaching to health, management, equipment and tourism. They learn what these jobs actually involve, the different people a professional works with (children, seniors, people with disabilities), and the core skills of the trade like teaching, training and keeping others safe, which helps them picture where their own path might lead.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.domain: Apports théoriques — Pratique physique et santé
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how movement and health connect: what health really means, how regular activity strengthens the heart, lungs and muscles while lifting mood and confidence, and why a sedentary lifestyle harms the body. They also pick up the habits of safe, healthy practice, such as proper warm-ups, recovery, good nutrition, and using simple tools to measure effort, giving them knowledge they can use for a lifetime of wellbeing.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.summary: Your teenager digs into how a sporting performance actually works: what makes a movement efficient, how forces and body levers combine, and how a given sport's technique, tactics and rules have evolved over time. They learn to observe a performance carefully and use video, sensors and apps to analyze and improve it, turning a gut sense of good or bad into precise, evidence-based judgment.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.domain: Attendus de fin de lycée pour l'enseignement de spécialité
clusters.summary: By the end of the specialty, your teenager can pull everything together: committing fully to reach their best physical performance, designing and evaluating their own activity project, building a reasoned argument about sports culture, and reflecting on their varied experiences to grow from them. These are the capstone goals that show they have become a thoughtful, capable practitioner rather than just a participant.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.summary: Your teenager builds the skill of analyzing physical activity rather than just doing it: keeping a training logbook, choosing the right indicators like heart rate or scores to read the effects of exercise, and understanding how sport fits into wider society and careers. Learning to interpret their own practice with evidence sharpens their thinking and feeds directly into their future study and job plans.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to talk about sport with precision and confidence: debating a topic using solid knowledge, describing and commenting on a performance with sharp observations, reporting clearly on how a project went, and presenting their own future plans grounded in real experience. These communication skills matter well beyond the gym, in any setting where they need to explain, persuade and present themselves.

clusters.subject: EPPCS
clusters.summary: Your teenager keeps developing as a hands-on practitioner: refining their movement skills across many activities, preparing and performing safely both alone and in a group, cooperating and drawing on each teammate's strengths, and even leading exercises or coaching a session for their peers. This blend of doing, working together and teaching others deepens both their ability and their confidence to guide.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Adapter ses déplacements à des environnements variés
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to move confidently through unfamiliar, safely-arranged settings, reading the ground, obstacles and water to adjust how they move. They also start to gain water autonomy, swimming a short distance without touching support. At this age it builds courage, body awareness and the basic self-reliance to explore new spaces.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Adapter ses déplacements à des environnements variés
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to move through varied and unfamiliar places, matching their technique to the terrain and pacing their effort so they keep enough energy to get back. They take on real safety rules, learn who to alert if something goes wrong, and work toward the national school swimming certificate. It teaches independence, caution and looking out for others.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Adapter ses déplacements à des environnements variés
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to plan and follow a route through natural settings on land, in water or in the air, thinking ahead about the trip and the return. They practise assessing risk, protecting the environment and keeping a partner safe alongside them. This age is about taking responsibility and making thoughtful decisions when the outcome is uncertain.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Agir, s'exprimer, comprendre à travers l'activité physique
clusters.summary: Your child is discovering what their body can do, running, jumping, throwing, balancing, moving in water, and handling objects of all shapes and weights. Through games, dances and sung rounds they learn to cooperate, take turns, express feelings with movement and keep an effort going a little longer. At this age it lays the foundation for coordination, confidence and being part of a group.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Apprendre à entretenir sa santé par une activité physique régulière
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to look after their own health by understanding sleep, food, hydration and regular movement, and applying these habits day to day. They start to gauge how active they really are and how hard to push without overdoing it. It helps them take early ownership of a healthy, balanced lifestyle.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Champ d'apprentissage 2 : adapter son déplacement à des environnements variés et incertains
clusters.summary: Your teen is learning to move efficiently and safely through changing, unpredictable terrain, choosing solid footing and following a route at their best level. They read maps, conditions and the environment to plan their own itinerary before and during the activity. This develops autonomy, judgement and safe decision-making when conditions can shift.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Champ d'apprentissage 3 : réaliser une prestation corporelle destinée à être vue et appréciée
clusters.summary: Your teen is learning to create and perform a routine, a choreography or an acrobatic sequence, built with real composition techniques and an expressive vocabulary of movement. They rehearse to commit fully and deliver their part in front of an audience. It builds creativity, focus and the confidence to be seen.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Champ d'apprentissage 3 : réaliser une prestation corporelle destinée à être vue et appréciée
clusters.summary: Your teen is refining how they compose and perform a routine meant to be watched and judged, whether aesthetic, acrobatic or expressive, playing with space, time and energy to stir emotion. They learn to manage risk while committing fully in front of an audience. This deepens artistic intent, self-control and stage presence.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Champ d'apprentissage 4 : conduire et maîtriser un affrontement collectif ou interindividuel
clusters.summary: Your teen is learning to compete in team and one-on-one sports by sizing up an opponent's strengths and weaknesses and adjusting their game plan as the match shifts. They put attacking and defending choices into practice while playing fair, accepting defeat and winning with humility. It sharpens tactical thinking, composure and sportsmanship.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Champ d'apprentissage 4 : conduire et maîtriser un affrontement collectif ou interindividuel
clusters.summary: Your teen is deepening how they read a contest, tracking the shifting balance of play to shape smart technical and tactical choices in attack and defence. They commit to winning while respecting and enforcing the rules and staying gracious in victory or defeat. This age is about combining strategy with genuine fair play.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Champ d'apprentissage 5 : réaliser et orienter son activité physique pour développer ses ressources et s'entretenir
clusters.summary: Your teen is learning to design their own training plan using knowledge of the body and effort, and to adjust parameters like intensity, load and recovery to get the results they want. They practise safe posture and controlled movement and keep a training logbook to track and steer their progress. It teaches self-directed, sustainable fitness.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Champ d'apprentissage 5 : réaliser et orienter son activité physique pour se développer et s'entretenir
clusters.summary: Your teen is building a coherent personal fitness project, drawing on how the body responds to effort and knowingly modulating training parameters to reach a chosen goal. They keep a training logbook to record, follow and refine their plan over time. This grows the autonomy to maintain their own health long-term.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Compétences méthodologiques et sociales (transversal aux champs d'apprentissage)
clusters.summary: Your teen is developing the teamwork and method skills that run across every sport, cooperating, taking on roles like referee, coach or spotter, and steering a personal or group project through planning and adjustment. They learn to read their body's signals to pace effort, warm up and recover, and use video and observation tools to improve. It builds responsibility, self-awareness and the discipline to keep getting better.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.summary: Your teen is learning the cross-cutting skills every physical activity needs, cooperating, supporting teammates, taking on roles, and running a project they plan and adjust as they go. They practise managing risk while staying committed, reading bodily sensations, warming up and recovering, and using observation tools to assess performance. It develops maturity, method and genuine solidarity.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.summary: Your child is broadening how they learn through sport, refining technique through repetition, describing movement in words, using digital tools to analyse actions, and reading effort indicators like heart rate. They also build sportsmanship, share and enforce rules, take on responsibilities and develop a thoughtful outlook on sport as a culture. This age connects physical skill with reflection, health and social awareness.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Conduire et maîtriser un affrontement collectif ou interindividuel
clusters.summary: Your child is learning the basics of games where they face an opponent, telling teammates from opponents, understanding the goal, and playing by the essential rules. They practise controlling their emotions, reading what an opponent does and adapting their actions to win. It teaches focus, cooperation and fair, rule-based play.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Conduire et maîtriser un affrontement collectif ou interindividuel
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to play team games by knowing when to attack or defend, cooperating with teammates and making simple tactical choices to create scoring chances. They keep up their effort for the whole game, respect opponents and referees, and accept and talk through the result. It builds teamwork, game-reading and good sportsmanship.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Conduire et maîtriser un affrontement collectif ou interindividuel
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to build a game plan that accounts for the balance of power, switching quickly between attacking and defending as play unfolds. They practise co-refereeing a match, staying loyal to teammates, respecting opponents and analysing the result honestly. This age blends tactics with responsibility and fair judgement.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Partager des règles, assumer des rôles et des responsabilités
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to take part fully in group activities while rotating through real roles, player, referee, judge, timekeeper, coach or observer. They come to understand the rules, respect them and help make sure others do too, all while keeping themselves and classmates safe. It grows responsibility, cooperation and respect for shared rules.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Produire une performance optimale, mesurable à une échéance donnée
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to run, jump and throw with purpose, telling apart goals like running fast versus far or jumping high versus long. They build coordination on both sides of the body and accept measuring their performance and comparing it in friendly competition. At this age it develops effort, focus and a healthy relationship with progress.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Produire une performance optimale, mesurable à une échéance donnée
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to combine athletic actions, run-and-jump or run-and-throw, and to use their run-up speed to go faster, higher and farther, including floating and balancing in water. They measure, record and compare their results and use cues to control their movement and effort. It links physical skill with observation and steady self-improvement.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: Produire une performance optimale, mesurable à une échéance donnée
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to mobilise their resources for the best possible performance by a set deadline, pacing effort with landmarks and physical cues. They warm up, follow a preparation program, and take on officiating roles like timekeeper, judge or measurer at events. This age combines personal performance with responsibility and preparation.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: S'exprimer devant les autres par une prestation artistique et/ou acrobatique
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to express feelings and intentions through movement, building a repertoire of artistic and acrobatic gestures while managing risk safely. They memorise steps and sequences, keep to a rhythm, synchronise with partners and accept showing themselves to others. It nurtures creativity, courage and the confidence to perform.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: S’approprier seul ou à plusieurs par la pratique, les méthodes et outils pour apprendre
clusters.summary: Your child is learning how to learn in sport, by doing, by watching others, and by analysing what worked and what didn't. They repeat a movement deliberately to make it stable, and use tools like video to observe and improve their own performance. It teaches method, self-observation and the value of practice.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: S’exprimer devant les autres par une prestation artistique et/ou acrobatique
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to use the expressive power of the body and their imagination to communicate an intention, creating and performing both acrobatic and artistic sequences in a small group. They accept performing in front of an audience and respect others' performances as a viewer. It develops creativity, teamwork and confidence on stage.

clusters.subject: EPS
clusters.domain: S’exprimer devant les autres par une prestation artistique et/ou acrobatique
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to compose an artistic or acrobatic sequence using simple composition and interpretation devices, committing to it while controlling risk and overcoming apprehension. They also learn to appraise performances thoughtfully, building a critical eye on their own work and that of others. This age blends creative expression with self-control and judgement.

clusters.subject: EVAR(S)
clusters.domain: Rencontrer les autres et construire des relations, s'y épanouir
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning what makes a relationship healthy rather than harmful, and that they get to choose who they let close and are always free to say no. They explore consent, mutual respect, and how to spot warning signs like coercion, harassment, or online pressure, alongside the practical side of protecting their health. Above all they learn that a victim of violence is never to blame, and where to find a trusted adult or support service when something feels wrong.

clusters.subject: EVAR(S)
clusters.domain: Se connaître, vivre et grandir avec son corps
clusters.summary: Your teenager is getting to know their own body, feelings, and emotions as they grow, and learning to name and manage what they feel. They explore the difference between an urge and a choice, come to understand attraction and their own developing identity, and learn that people differ in sex, gender, and orientation, all deserving respect. This helps them build a calmer, more confident relationship with themselves and with others.

clusters.subject: EVAR(S)
clusters.domain: Trouver sa place dans la société, y être libre et responsable
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to take their place among others as a free and responsible person, standing up for equality and against discrimination. They build a critical eye for the messages and images around them, learning to question stereotypes, decode advertising and pornography, and tell what respects people from what demeans them. They also learn how to react when someone is being harassed or abused, including how to report it and use helplines like 3018.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.domain: 1 — Une longue histoire de la matière
clusters.summary: Your teenager steps back to see the big picture of science itself: how ordinary matter grew more and more complex until life appeared, and why telling the living apart from the non-living is harder than it sounds. They also learn that scientific ideas are not fixed truths but explanations that get debated, refined, and sometimes overturned, built up by observing the Universe at every scale from atoms to galaxies. It is a grounding in how science actually thinks and changes its mind.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager discovers that the air we breathe today was made by living things: early bacteria filled the atmosphere with oxygen billions of years ago, completely reshaping the planet. They see how water made life possible and how the ozone layer high above us shields us from harmful rays, and they look critically at the story of the 'ozone hole' to understand how science and global decisions come together. It connects the chemistry of the atmosphere to the survival of life.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.domain: 1.1 — Un niveau d'organisation : les éléments chimiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns that every atom in their body was forged inside stars, and that the mix of chemical elements in the Universe, on Earth, and in living things is strikingly different. They meet radioactivity: why some atomic nuclei are unstable, how 'half-life' lets scientists date rocks and fossils, and how these same materials are used carefully in medicine. It links the origin of matter to real tools for measuring the age of the world.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager explores how the atoms in a crystal, like ordinary salt, are stacked in a strict, repeating order, and how that hidden microscopic arrangement decides everyday properties such as hardness and density. They compare orderly crystals with disordered solids like glass and discover that living things build crystals too, in shells, bones, and stones. It shows how invisible structure shapes the materials all around us.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager digs into how Earth's climate really works: the greenhouse effect, the feedback loops that amplify warming as ice melts, and the huge role the ocean plays in soaking up heat and slowing change. They learn to read the graphs and indicators scientists use, tell climate apart from day-to-day weather, and spot the flawed reasoning behind climate scepticism. It gives them the tools to understand one of the defining challenges of their generation.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how scientists forecast the climate of the coming century by turning the planet's key mechanisms into computer models, and why those models broadly agree on rising temperatures and seas. They see how human activity drives recent warming, the difference between reducing causes and adapting to consequences, and how a correlation is not the same as a proven cause. It helps them read climate news with a sharper, more critical eye.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.domain: 1.3 — Une structure complexe : la cellule vivante
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns that every living thing is built from cells, tiny water-filled units bounded by a membrane where the chemistry of life takes place. They follow how the microscope revealed this hidden world and led to cell theory, and they wrestle with genuinely open questions like whether a virus counts as alive. It gives them the fundamental building block that all of biology rests on.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager discovers how two centuries of physics turned into the electricity that powers daily life: alternators that convert motion into current with almost no waste, and solar panels that turn sunlight directly into power. They learn enough about how a semiconductor absorbs light to reason about whether solar panels make sense on a home. It ties abstract physics to real energy choices.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns where the Sun's energy comes from, nuclear fusion deep in its core, and how that energy reaches us as radiation that warms the whole planet. They discover the neat rule that a glowing object's colour reveals its temperature, and why the Sun's power on the ground depends on the time of day and the seasons. It explains the energy source behind almost everything alive on Earth.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.domain: 2.2 — Conversion et transport de l'énergie électrique
clusters.summary: Your teenager follows electricity from its source to the wall socket: how different resources are converted into power, why it travels at very high voltage to avoid wasting energy as heat, and how the grid must constantly balance supply and demand. They also weigh the environmental cost of building and running power plants, since even clean methods leave a footprint. It reveals the hidden system behind flipping a light switch.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns the energy budget that sets Earth's temperature: sunlight comes in, some bounces straight back depending on how reflective the surface is, and the rest is absorbed and later released as heat. They see how the atmosphere traps part of that outgoing warmth, the greenhouse effect, keeping the planet livable. It gives the physics foundation for understanding climate itself.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.domain: 2.3 — De la conversion biologique de l'énergie solaire par la photosynthèse à l'énergie nécessaire à tous les êtres vivants
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how plants capture a sliver of sunlight through photosynthesis to build living matter, and how that stored energy ends up feeding every creature, including us, through what we eat. They connect this to their own body, working out how food provides energy and why a balanced diet matters for health. It links the Sun, the food chain, and personal wellbeing in one thread.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.domain: 2.3 — Énergie, choix de développement et futur climatique
clusters.summary: Your teenager examines the big picture of how humanity uses energy: where it comes from, how burning fossil fuels adds carbon dioxide and pollutes the air, and how the carbon cycle moves carbon between the atmosphere, oceans, and living things. They learn to estimate a carbon footprint and weigh the trade-offs behind the ecological transition. It equips them to think seriously about energy and climate choices.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.domain: 2.4 — Une diversité de sources d'énergie utilisables par l'Humanité
clusters.summary: Your teenager surveys the energy sources humanity can draw on, learning that fossil fuels are ancient sunlight stored in buried organic matter over millions of years, while biomass and solar-linked flows renew far faster. They learn to compare how long a source takes to form against how fast we use it, which is the heart of what 'renewable' really means. It builds clear thinking about the limits of our resources.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how scientists actually measure the living world, estimating how many animals are in a population or how diverse an ecosystem is through clever sampling methods, and using probability to predict how genes spread across generations. They see why small, fragmented populations lose genetic richness and how human and ecosystem health are bound together in the 'One Health' idea. It combines biology with real statistical reasoning.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager revisits a fact we take for granted, that the Earth is a sphere, and learns the elegant proofs, from ancient Greece to today, that establish it even though the ground looks flat. They work out practical things like how far the horizon is and how any point on the globe is pinned down by latitude and longitude. It turns a familiar idea into a piece of real geometry and history.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager explores where humans come from: how we sit among the primates, closest to the great apes, and how fossils millions of years old trace a bushy family tree marked by walking upright. They learn to read that tree from shared physical features and discover that some traits, like language and habits, pass on without genes at all. It gives an evidence-based view of our own origins.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager places Earth in the cosmos: circling the Sun, sitting in the narrow 'habitable zone' where water can stay liquid, with a Moon whose phases they finally learn to explain. They also revisit the historic, hard-fought shift from an Earth-centred to a Sun-centred view of the heavens, and consider why fresh water is so unevenly shared across the planet. It connects astronomy to the conditions that make life here possible.

clusters.subject: Enseignement scientifique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns the maths behind population growth: the difference between steady, straight-line increase and runaway exponential growth, and how to work out how long a population takes to double. They test simple models like Malthus's against real data and see both their power and their limits when forecasting the world's future population and food supply. It shows how mathematics helps answer pressing real-world questions.
