clusters.summary: Your child is learning to model everyday situations with maths: recognising whether a problem is about adding, multiplying or proportion, and seeing when a real setup can be described with geometric relationships. This ability to translate life into maths is at the core of problem solving.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Modéliser le hasard, calculer des probabilités
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to model randomness, building a probability model from observed frequencies while keeping model and reality distinct. Using trees, tables and set language for events, they compute probabilities for experiments with several stages, a foundation for statistics and decision-making.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is committing key number facts to memory: the addition and multiplication tables in both directions, doubles and halves, and handy facts like the multiples of 25. Recalling these instantly, even filling in missing numbers, makes all later calculation faster and easier.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is strengthening their grasp of decimal numbers: reading and writing them in several ways, knowing place value down to thousandths, and linking simple fractions to their decimal form. They use decimals to express measurements, connecting numbers to the quantities they describe.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is consolidating whole numbers and calculation: understanding place value and the units of numeration, representing numbers in many ways, and solving everyday problems that lead to the four operations. Linking numbers to measured quantities makes the maths feel real and useful.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is bringing together large whole numbers, fractions and decimals, place value, and the written and mental methods for the four operations. They learn the properties of operations as strategies, divisibility rules, and how to solve multi-step problems, a broad consolidation of their number work.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is broadening the numbers they work with to include negatives, fractions and scientific notation, and calculating fluently with all of them. They meet powers, square roots and orders of magnitude, tools for handling both the very large and the very small in science.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is working with fractions more flexibly: adding those with the same denominator, comparing and ordering them, and using fractions as operators to share a quantity. They handle fractions both below and above one, preparing them for the fuller fraction work of secondary school.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to read and build simple data displays, such as bar charts and two-way tables, and to pull information from them to answer questions. They even run a small survey from start to finish, gathering and organising data themselves, an early feel for how information is collected.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to collect, organise and present data as tables, bar and pie charts and graphs, and to read them to solve problems. They plan and carry out surveys and plot how one quantity changes with another, such as temperature over time, building real data-handling skills.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Organisation et gestion de données, fonctions
clusters.summary: Your teenager is bringing together data handling, functions and proportionality: reading and displaying data, recognising the straight-line graphs of linear and affine functions, and applying percentages and scales. They also meet the first ideas of probability, connecting several key strands of maths.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is studying right angles and distances in three-dimensional space using the scalar product and coordinates. They find the closest point on a line or plane, work with normal vectors and spheres, and solve problems about lengths, angles and volumes, advanced spatial geometry.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning conditional probability, how the chance of one event changes once you know another has happened, read from two-way tables and probability trees. They also study independent events, ideas essential for reasoning correctly about risk, testing and real-world uncertainty.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is learning what a percentage means, a part out of a hundred, starting from familiar fractions like a half and a quarter. They apply percentages to quantities and express a proportion as a percentage, one of the most useful everyday maths skills for shopping, discounts and statistics.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning about primitives (reversing differentiation) and differential equations, which describe how a quantity changes in terms of itself. They solve basic equations and use numerical methods, the mathematics behind population growth, cooling, and countless models in science and engineering.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is deepening conditional probability and independence, using weighted probability trees and the law of total probability to compute chances in layered situations. They also meet the Monte-Carlo method, estimating quantities through random sampling, powerful reasoning for statistics and simulation.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to reason mathematically: justifying their claims, checking whether information is valid, and organising several steps into a coherent argument. In geometry they move from just looking to checking properties with instruments, building the logic that underpins proof.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is describing lines, planes and spheres in space with equations and parametric forms, and solving configuration problems with systems of equations. They compute projections and intersections, the coordinate-based geometry that drives computer graphics, navigation and 3D design.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to represent maths in helpful ways: analysing a figure through its outline, lines and points, using codes on figures, and producing different pictures of fractions and decimals. Drawings, sketches and diagrams become tools for thinking through a problem.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Représenter algébriquement et graphiquement les fonctions
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to represent functions both by formula and by graph, understanding the domain, reading the curve, and using it to solve equations and inequalities. They also study even and odd functions and sign tables, core techniques for analysing how quantities relate.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Représenter et caractériser les droites du plan
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to describe straight lines with equations, finding a line from two points or from a point and a slope, and deciding whether lines are parallel or where they cross. They connect slope, direction vector and alignment, the algebra of lines that underlies coordinate geometry.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is solving geometry problems by calculating lengths, angles, areas and volumes, and by proving classic results such as the concurrence of a triangle's altitudes. They use trigonometric formulas and tackle optimisation, applying reasoning to both simple and complex figures.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is solving proportionality problems using rates of change, multiplying coefficients and ratios, and applying them to percentages, scales and enlargements. Recognising when a situation is proportional and using a linking formula are practical skills for everyday and financial decisions.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Résoudre des problèmes impliquant des longueurs, des masses, des contenances, des durées, des prix
clusters.summary: Your child is solving real problems involving lengths, masses, capacities, durations and prices, converting between everyday units and choosing the right operation. Handling money in euros and centimes and finding perimeters ties their arithmetic firmly to daily life.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Se constituer un répertoire de fonctions de référence
clusters.summary: Your teenager is building a toolkit of key reference functions, the square, inverse, square-root and cube functions, knowing the shape of each curve and how they compare. They solve equations and inequalities with these functions, a foundation they will draw on throughout advanced maths.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Se familiariser avec les motifs organisés
clusters.summary: Your young child is exploring repeating patterns, copying them, continuing them from a start, and spotting the odd element that breaks the rhythm. They grasp that a pattern like AB has an underlying structure whatever the objects, and invent their own, an early form of algebraic thinking through play.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how averages and spread behave when random quantities are added together, including that expectations always add and, for independent variables, variances add too. They apply this to samples and to the binomial law, key tools for statistics and understanding sampling.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Succession d'épreuves indépendantes, schéma de Bernoulli
clusters.summary: Your teenager is studying repeated success-or-failure trials and the binomial distribution that counts the successes, using probability trees. They model real situations and solve threshold problems, and meet related laws for the first success and for rare events, central ideas in probability and statistics.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is studying sequences and their limits, learning when a sequence settles toward a value or grows without bound, and proving results by induction. They use comparison and squeeze theorems and approximate numbers like pi and the square root of 2, rigorous tools at the heart of analysis.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning arithmetic and geometric sequences, which model steady and percentage-based change, and how to define a sequence explicitly, by recurrence or by algorithm. They compute terms and sums and use sequences to model real evolving situations, connecting patterns to functions.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is formalising reflection symmetry, learning that it preserves distances and angles and maps points, lines and segments to matching ones. They learn the precise definition of a point's mirror image and construct symmetric figures with proper instruments, sharpening their geometric accuracy.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your child is studying triangles: knowing that their three angles add up to 180 degrees, the special angle properties of isosceles and equilateral triangles, and how to construct triangles accurately from given data. They also meet the circumscribed circle and why a triangle's perpendicular bisectors meet at one point.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is using algebra fluently: calculating with powers, roots and fractions, choosing whether an expression is more useful expanded or factored, and applying the remarkable identities. They rearrange formulas to isolate a variable and solve and model with inequalities, versatile problem-solving tools.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Utiliser les nombres pour résoudre des problèmes
clusters.summary: Your young child is using numbers to solve little problems, acting them out with objects, counting on their fingers, and sharing objects fairly. Finding a missing part of a whole or a total from equal groups gives them their first real sense that numbers are useful for figuring things out.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Utiliser les notions de multiple, diviseur et de nombre premier
clusters.summary: Your teenager is working with multiples, divisors and prime numbers, testing divisibility and primality and reducing fractions to lowest terms. They prove general facts, such as that the square of an odd number is odd, learning to reason rigorously about whole numbers.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Utiliser les variables et les instructions élémentaires
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning the building blocks of programming: variables and assignment, data types, conditional statements, and both counted ('for') and open-ended ('while') loops. They read, modify and write short programs, gaining genuine coding fundamentals that carry across languages.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Utiliser l’information chiffrée et statistique descriptive
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to interpret figures and statistics: distinguishing absolute from relative change, combining successive percentage changes, and comparing data series with measures of average and spread. Working with percentages of percentages and weighted means equips them to read data critically.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Utiliser ses connaissances en numération pour calculer mentalement
clusters.summary: Your child is using their understanding of place value to calculate in their head: adding or subtracting 1, 2, 10 or whole tens, and multiplying by 10 or 100. Seeing that multiplying by ten shifts each digit up a rank connects mental maths to how numbers are built.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning about random variables, which put a number on the outcome of a chance experiment, and how to describe them with expectation, variance and standard deviation. They model situations, simulate them in Python, and use expectation to make fair decisions, such as a fair stake in a game.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning the language of mathematical reasoning: sets and their operations, logical connectives, quantifiers, and implication and equivalence. They meet proof methods like counter-examples, cases and contradiction, the precise thinking that all rigorous maths depends on.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is refining their command of logic and proof: forming the converse, contrapositive and negation of statements, distinguishing necessary from sufficient conditions, and reasoning by cases or contradiction. They also clarify the status of letters and equalities, tightening their mathematical writing.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning about sampling: how a random sample lets you estimate a probability or a proportion, and the law of large numbers that makes this reliable when the sample is big. Simulating samples in Python shows them how close the estimate usually gets, the basis of polling and surveys.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Équations, fonctions polynômes du second degré
clusters.summary: Your teenager is mastering quadratics, the degree-two functions whose graphs are parabolas, using the canonical form and discriminant to solve equations, factor, and determine sign. Choosing the right form for each problem and reading roots from factored form are key algebra skills for further study.

clusters.subject: Mathématiques
clusters.domain: Étudier les variations et les extremums d’une fonction
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to study how a function rises and falls, reading its maximums and minimums from a graph and moving fluently between a curve and its variation table. They connect the slope of a straight line to its direction and use software and simple algorithms to explore these variations.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to design step-by-step methods a computer can follow, from everyday tasks like finding the largest value in a list or putting items in order, to cleverer strategies for searching, finding the shortest path through a network, or breaking a big problem into smaller pieces. Just as importantly, they learn to reason about why a method actually works and always finishes. These are the core problem-solving skills behind almost every piece of software they use.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.summary: Your teenager is looking under the hood of a computer to see how the processor, memory, and operating system actually cooperate to run a program, and how sensors and controls let a machine react to the real world. They also get hands-on with the command line and with wiring up a small network so devices can talk to each other. It demystifies the everyday devices they rely on and shows there is real engineering, not magic, inside.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.domain: Architectures matérielles, systèmes d'exploitation et réseaux
clusters.summary: In their final year your teenager digs deeper into how a computer juggles many tasks at once and how data finds its way across a network from one machine to another. They also meet the basics of encryption, the idea of a shared or public key that keeps online messages private. This is the foundation for understanding both how modern systems stay fast and how the internet keeps information secure.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the large collections of information behind websites and apps are organised into linked tables, and how to ask precise questions of that data using the SQL language to find, add, or update records. They also learn to spot poorly designed structures that lead to errors or duplicated information. It is one of the most directly useful skills in almost any workplace that handles data.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.domain: Interactions entre l'homme et la machine sur le Web
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning what really happens when they fill in a web form and click submit: which work is done by their own browser, which is handled by a distant server, and how the two exchange information. They also learn when a connection is safely encrypted and why that matters. This helps them become a more informed, security-aware user and creator of the web pages they interact with daily.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.summary: Your teenager is deepening their programming craft, learning to write clear reusable code, define exactly what each piece is meant to do, test it thoroughly, and track down bugs methodically. They also step back to see the bigger picture, comparing different families of programming languages and even touching on deep ideas about the limits of what computers can ever compute. It builds the disciplined, careful habits of a genuine software developer.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to organise information inside a program using handy containers such as lists, tables, and dictionaries that pair a name with a value, like a contact book. These are the everyday building blocks programmers reach for to store and work with real-world data. Choosing the right container is a small but essential skill that makes programs simpler and faster.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.domain: Représentation des données : types et valeurs de base
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering how everything inside a computer, from whole numbers and decimals to letters and symbols, is ultimately stored as ones and zeros. They learn to convert between number systems, see why some decimal values can never be stored exactly, and how text encoding lets different languages and emojis appear on screen. It reveals the surprisingly logical foundations beneath every digital device.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to pick the right way to organise data for a given problem, using structures such as graphs for networks and connections, or trees for anything with a nested hierarchy like a file system or family tree. They also meet the object-oriented style of programming that groups data and actions together. Choosing a well-suited structure is what separates clumsy programs from elegant, efficient ones.

clusters.subject: NSI
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to work with real datasets stored as tables, much like a spreadsheet, loading them from files and then filtering, sorting, and combining rows to answer questions. This is the everyday work of turning raw data into useful insight. It is a practical, transferable skill that underpins data analysis in science, business, and journalism alike.

clusters.subject: Philosophie
clusters.summary: Your teenager takes on the big questions that thinkers have wrestled with for centuries: what freedom really means, whether happiness is a goal or a duty, what makes something true, and how justice, work, art, and the state shape our lives. Rather than memorizing answers, they learn to think carefully and honestly about ideas that touch everyone, which sharpens their judgement for the choices adult life will bring.

clusters.subject: Philosophie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how to build a real argument: asking the right question, backing up what they claim with reasons, drawing on great texts they have read, and weighing opposing views fairly before deciding. They also learn to turn a critical eye on their own opinions instead of taking them for granted, a habit that serves them in any subject and in everyday decisions.

clusters.subject: Philosophie
clusters.summary: Your teenager picks up a toolkit of precise distinctions that make thinking clearer, such as the difference between believing and knowing, between what is legal and what is truly fair, or between an example and a genuine proof. These pairings give them a sharper vocabulary for reasoning, helping them notice when an argument is solid and when it only sounds convincing.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Analyser un système chimique par des méthodes physiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to figure out what an unknown substance is, and how much of it is in a solution, using physical instruments rather than chemical reactions. They shine light through samples, measure how well a liquid conducts electricity, and use pH readings, applying trusted physical laws to turn those measurements into real answers.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Analyser un système par des méthodes chimiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to measure the exact concentration of a solution by slowly adding a reactant until a reaction is complete, watching for a colour change or a signal that marks the tipping point. They also practise preparing solutions to a precise strength, choosing the right lab glassware, which is a core skill for careful chemistry.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager builds the reasoning habits of a scientist: forming a hypothesis, planning an experiment to test it, and sensibly estimating whether a result is the right size before trusting it. They also learn to pick the right model or law for a situation and use it to make predictions, skills that carry across all their science work.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how energy explains movement: why a falling object speeds up, how energy stored by height turns into motion, and why total mechanical energy stays constant when there is no friction. Using phones and tracking software to follow a moving object, they connect the physics to everyday examples like rollercoasters and dropped balls.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns what electric current really is, a flow of tiny charges, and how electrical energy is delivered, used, and partly wasted as heat in the devices we use every day. They study why chargers and appliances are never perfectly efficient and get a feel for how much power common gadgets actually draw.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to recognise a real chemical change and describe it precisely by writing and balancing its reaction equation. Through hands-on work like heating, chromatography, and tracking temperature, they discover ideas such as whether a reaction gives off or absorbs heat, and that a substance made in the lab can be identical to one found in nature.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager gets a first look at the heart of the atom, learning what isotopes are and how reactions in the nucleus power both the Sun and nuclear plants. They learn to tell apart a physical, chemical, or nuclear change, which frames big questions about energy in the world around them.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager builds practical lab competence: setting up equipment to record and process measurements, using software to model and analyse data, and, importantly, working safely and disposing of chemicals responsibly. These are the everyday professional habits behind every experiment they run.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Capacités expérimentales — Constitution et transformations de la matière
clusters.summary: Your teenager practises the hands-on techniques chemists use to purify and identify substances: filtering, washing, heating under reflux, distilling, and running chromatography. They also learn to measure the temperature at which something melts or boils and to picture molecules in three dimensions using models and software.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Comparer la force des acides et des bases
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns why some acids and bases are strong and others weak, and how to predict what a solution actually contains. They meet everyday chemicals like vinegar, bleach, and ammonia, work with pH and buffer solutions, and use diagrams and calculations to reason about acidity with real precision.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Constitution et transformations de la matière
clusters.summary: Your teenager develops a wide toolkit of lab skills, from building a battery and running a titration to extracting a substance and measuring it with light. Handling real instruments and following procedures carefully, they learn to determine concentrations and separate mixtures the way a working chemist would.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: De la structure des entités aux propriétés physiques de la matière
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how a molecule's shape and structure explain the everyday properties of matter: why some substances dissolve in water and others do not, why solids hold together, and how soap manages to clean grease. They draw molecular structures and use them to predict real behaviour, connecting the invisible world of atoms to things they can see.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Des signaux pour observer et communiquer
clusters.summary: Your child learns how light and sound carry information to our eyes and ears, why light travels in straight lines, and why we can see objects that make their own light and others that only reflect it. They explore how sound travels and how fast, and even meet the mind-stretching idea of the light-year as a way to measure the vast distances between stars.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how gases and liquids behave when still, discovering why pressure increases the deeper you go underwater and how squeezing a gas raises its pressure. They connect these everyday effects to simple laws they can test in the lab, building intuition about the fluids all around us.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Description et caractérisation de la matière à l'échelle macroscopique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to describe and identify substances by measurable properties like density and by simple chemical tests for water, oxygen, or carbon dioxide. They tell pure substances from mixtures, work out what a mixture is made of, and get comfortable measuring masses and volumes accurately with lab glassware.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Interactions fondamentales et introduction à la notion de champ
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns about the invisible forces that push and pull charged objects, and sees the elegant parallel between electric attraction and gravity, which both weaken with distance in the same way. They meet the powerful idea of a field, a way to map how a force acts through empty space, which underlies much of modern physics.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager studies how energy moves and changes form, from heat flowing between objects to the energy balance that sets the Earth's temperature and drives the greenhouse effect. They build circuits, model how things heat and cool over time, and connect these ideas to real concerns like climate and energy use.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: L'énergie, ses transferts et ses conversions
clusters.summary: Your child learns the different forms energy can take and the crucial rule that energy is never lost, only transferred or transformed. Building real circuits, they discover how current, voltage, and power work, meet Ohm's law, and learn to trace energy through a system from its source to its final use.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: La lumière : images et couleurs — Images et couleurs
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how colour works, both in the eye, which senses colour through three types of receptor, and in the world, where mixing lights or filters produces the colours we see. They also study how a lens forms an image, the physics behind cameras, projectors, and the human eye itself.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: La lumière : images et couleurs — Modèles ondulatoire et particulaire de la lumière
clusters.summary: Your teenager discovers that light has two faces: it behaves as a wave spread across a whole spectrum, from radio to X-rays, and as a stream of tiny energy packets called photons. They learn how atoms absorb and emit specific colours of light, which explains everything from neon signs to how we read the composition of distant stars.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns that no measurement is ever perfectly exact, and how real scientists honestly report that uncertainty. They repeat measurements, look at how the results spread, and learn to compare a result against a reference value, a rigorous habit that separates careful science from guesswork.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Modélisation de la matière à l'échelle microscopique
clusters.summary: Your teenager zooms into the atom, learning how it is built, how electrons are arranged, and how that arrangement explains the layout of the periodic table. They learn why atoms form ions and bonds to become more stable, and how chemists count atoms in bulk using the mole, connecting the tiny scale to amounts we can weigh.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Modéliser l'évolution temporelle d'un système, siège d'une transformation nucléaire
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how unstable atomic nuclei decay over time and how scientists use that steady, predictable decay to date ancient rocks and archaeological finds. They also study how we protect ourselves from radiation and how radioactivity is put to work in medicine, tying deep physics to real-world safety and health.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your child learns to describe how things move, by their path and their speed, and to see forces as the pushes and pulls that cause or change motion. They discover that whether something is moving depends on your point of view, and meet gravity as the force that gives objects their weight.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager applies real physics to motion, using Newton's laws to link forces and acceleration, analysing projectiles and orbiting planets, and testing Kepler's laws with genuine astronomical data. With video tracking, sensors, and calculus, they move from describing motion to predicting it, the same reasoning used to launch satellites.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Mouvement et interactions — 1. Décrire un mouvement
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to describe motion precisely: choosing a reference point, tracking an object frame by frame from video, and drawing velocity vectors to show not just how fast but in which direction something moves. They start using programming to plot positions and velocities, a modern take on a classic physics skill.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Mouvement et interactions — 2. Modéliser une action sur un système
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to model every push and pull on an object as a force, drawn as an arrow with a size and direction, whether it acts by contact or at a distance like gravity. They meet the key principle that forces always come in pairs: when one object pushes another, it gets pushed back just as hard.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager explores waves and signals in depth, from ripples and sound to light, discovering effects like diffraction, interference, and the Doppler shift that changes a siren's pitch as it passes. They build optical instruments like telescopes, work with sensors and circuits, and see how these ideas power cameras, solar cells, and speed detection.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager studies how disturbances travel through a medium as waves, learning to measure a wave's speed, wavelength, and period and the neat relationship connecting them. They use this to work out how far away a source is from the time a wave takes to arrive, the principle behind sonar and locating a thunderstorm.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Organisation et transformations de la matière
clusters.summary: Your child explores what everything is made of, from the three states of matter and the atoms inside them to the idea that the same elements build both the Earth and the stars. They learn about chemical changes like combustion and rusting, measure pH and density, and place these ideas on the grand scale of the Universe.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your child learns to think and work like a scientist: telling apart questions that can be tested from mere opinions, forming hypotheses, designing experiments, and drawing well-argued conclusions from results. These investigative habits underpin all their science learning and reward genuine curiosity.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your child learns to communicate science clearly, using precise French to report what they observe and moving fluently between words, tables, graphs, and diagrams. They also practise reading scientific documents and defending a viewpoint with evidence in a debate, skills that matter far beyond the science classroom.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Propriétés physico-chimiques, synthèses et combustions d'espèces chimiques organiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager steps into organic chemistry, learning to recognise families of carbon-based molecules, name them, and identify them using infrared light. They carry out real syntheses, measuring the yield, and study the energy released when fuels burn, while weighing the environmental impact and the principles of greener chemistry.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Prévoir le sens de l'évolution spontanée d'un système chimique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to predict which way a chemical reaction will naturally go and why some reactions stop before finishing rather than running to completion. Building and testing real batteries, they see how spontaneous chemical change produces electricity, and learn to calculate how much charge a battery can deliver.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: S'approprier des outils et des méthodes
clusters.summary: Your child learns the practical methods behind good science work: researching reliable sources, planning and organising an experiment, keeping clear records, and using digital tools to share findings. These organisational and research skills set them up to work independently and carefully.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how electrical circuits carry information and how sensors turn the physical world into signals a device can read, for example a resistor whose value changes with temperature or light. They measure voltage and current, apply Ohm's law, and see how the sensors in everyday gadgets and microcontrollers actually work.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Suivi de l'évolution d'un système, siège d'une transformation
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to track a chemical reaction from start to finish, working out exactly how much of each substance is present at the end using a bookkeeping tool called a table of advancement. They measure concentrations by titration and by colour, and meet electron-transfer reactions, the chemistry behind batteries and corrosion.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Suivre et modéliser l'évolution temporelle d'un système siège d'une transformation chimique
clusters.summary: Your teenager studies how fast reactions happen and what makes them speed up or slow down, such as temperature, concentration, and catalysts. They picture reactions as countless tiny collisions between molecules, track a reaction's progress over time, and use programming to test whether it follows a predictable rate law.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns the physics of changes of state, like melting and boiling, and the energy each one takes in or gives off. They learn to tell melting apart from dissolving, decide whether a change heats or cools its surroundings, and write it out as an equation, explaining everyday things like ice cubes and steam.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how we see and how light behaves: how lenses form images, how the eye works like a lens and a screen, and how a prism splits white light into a rainbow. They study reflection and refraction, learn what gives a colour its wavelength, and see how the spectrum of light reveals the temperature of a glowing object.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.domain: Élaborer des stratégies en synthèse organique
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to plan multi-step strategies for building complex molecules, classifying reactions and reshaping a molecule step by step to reach a target. They meet clever techniques like protecting part of a molecule while working on another, and study polymers, the long-chain molecules behind plastics and many everyday materials.

clusters.subject: Physique-chimie
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how sound is made when something vibrates, why it needs air or another material to travel, and how its frequency sets the pitch we hear. Using microphones and software, they measure real sound signals and learn about the decibel scale and why loud sounds can damage hearing, connecting physics to their own health.

clusters.subject: Questionner le monde
clusters.summary: Your child is discovering what makes something alive, from plants and animals to their own body, learning what living things need to grow, how they feed one another, and how bodies move, change and stay healthy. They start to see themselves as part of nature and pick up everyday habits like eating well, moving, resting and keeping clean that matter for a lifetime.

clusters.subject: Questionner le monde
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to think and work like a young scientist: asking questions, observing carefully, trying things out and then explaining what they found in clear French, out loud and in writing. Alongside this they begin using documents and simple digital tools to find information, and take on small responsible habits toward the planet, like sorting waste.

clusters.subject: Questionner le monde
clusters.summary: Your child is exploring how people live and organise the spaces around them, from their own neighbourhood or village to different landscapes and far-off places. By comparing how life differs across time periods and cultures, and reading photos and maps, they start to understand that the way people eat, dress and travel is shaped by where and when they live.

clusters.subject: Questionner le monde
clusters.summary: Your child is taking a first look at how everyday objects work and what they are for, even building simple ones like small electric circuits by following a diagram. They learn the basics of electricity, which materials let current pass, and begin finding their way around a computer, connecting hands-on making with the technology that surrounds them.

clusters.subject: Questionner le monde
clusters.summary: Your child is getting curious about what everything is made of, discovering that matter comes as solids, liquids and gases, and that even air is real stuff that takes up space. Through simple experiments with water and air, they watch water freeze and melt and start measuring things, which builds the habit of testing ideas rather than just guessing.

clusters.subject: Questionner le monde
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to find their bearings in space, starting from familiar places and stretching out to their region, France, Europe, the continents and oceans, and even Earth's place among the stars. They pick up the words for position and direction and learn to read maps, plans and globes, building a nested sense of where they fit in the wider world.

clusters.subject: Questionner le monde
clusters.summary: Your child is building a sense of time, from the rhythms of day and night, weeks and seasons to reading a clock and calendar and ordering events as before or after. They learn the units of time and how they fit together, and begin placing family memories and a few historical moments on a timeline, grasping that time always moves forward and never back.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning what unemployment really means, how it is measured, and why it happens. They explore whether joblessness comes from a slow economy or from deeper mismatches between workers and jobs, and they weigh the main tools governments use to bring it down. This helps them make sense of headlines and debates about work in their own future.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.domain: Maîtrise des données quantitatives et des représentations graphiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is building the numerical toolkit that runs through every economics and sociology topic: reading and drawing charts, working out averages and medians, and computing percentages, rates of change and index numbers. They learn to tell a nominal figure from one adjusted for rising prices, and to spot when two things move together without one actually causing the other. These skills let them read data critically rather than take a statistic at face value.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.domain: Quelles inégalités sont compatibles avec les différentes conceptions de la justice sociale ?
clusters.summary: Your teenager is exploring what a fair society looks like, comparing ideas of equal rights, equal opportunities and equal outcomes. They learn how inequality has evolved over the past century, how governments try to reduce it, and the standard tools used to measure gaps between rich and poor. This gives them a thoughtful way to reason about a debate they will hear all their lives.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.domain: Quelles mutations du travail et de l'emploi ?
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the world of work has changed, from rigid assembly-line organization to more flexible ways of working. They untangle related ideas like work, activity, employment and unemployment, and think about what makes a job a good one, from pay and security to whether it helps someone feel part of society. It prepares them to think clearly about the kind of work they might want.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.domain: Quels sont les caractéristiques contemporaines et les facteurs de la mobilité sociale ?
clusters.summary: Your teenager is studying social mobility: whether children tend to end up better off, worse off, or similar to their parents. They look at how schooling, family background and a changing job market shape those chances, and how sociologists use mobility tables to measure them. This helps them understand how open or closed their own society really is.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.domain: Quels sont les fondements du commerce international et de l'internationalisation de la production ?
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning why countries trade with one another and why companies spread their production across the globe. They discover the idea of comparative advantage, follow how a single product can be made in many countries, and weigh the winners and losers of free trade. This turns 'made in' labels and news about globalization into something they can genuinely reason about.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.domain: Quels sont les sources et les défis de la croissance économique ?
clusters.summary: Your teenager is exploring what makes an economy grow: investment, innovation and the institutions that encourage people to take risks and create. They meet the idea that innovation both creates and destroys, and they confront the tension between endless growth and the planet's ecological limits. It gives them a grounded view of one of the biggest questions of their generation.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.summary: Your teenager is being introduced to what the social sciences are and how they work. They see the core question each field asks, learn that researchers rely on surveys, data and simplified models of reality, and practise the crucial habit of not confusing correlation with genuine cause. This first step teaches them to think like a social scientist rather than trust hunches.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.summary: Your teenager is looking at how education connects to work and pay, seeing why staying in school can be thought of as an investment that tends to pay off in better job prospects. They also learn that qualifications don't fully explain wages, that a lack of them can lead to unemployment, and that access to diplomas is not equally open to everyone. It links their studies to real choices about their future.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.domain: Regards croisés — la gestion des risques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how people and societies deal with life's risks, such as illness, accident, job loss and old age. They see who helps manage these risks, from families and insurers to public social protection, and weigh the upsides and pitfalls of sharing risk together. This helps them understand why systems like health insurance and pensions exist.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.summary: Your teenager is meeting the basics of economics: who produces what, how markets set prices where supply meets demand, and how we measure a country's output with GDP and a firm's success with figures like turnover and profit. They also start questioning these measures, learning that GDP hides inequalities and that growth bumps against ecological limits. These foundations make the economic world visible and understandable.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.summary: Your teenager is going deeper into how markets work and where they fail, from monopolies and pollution to hidden information between buyers and sellers. They learn how buyers and sellers both gain from trade, when governments step in to fix market failures, and how money, saving, borrowing and public budgets fit together. It sharpens their grasp of both the power and the limits of markets.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the economy is financed: how those who need money connect with those who have it, how competition ranges from many sellers to a single one, and how banks and the central bank actually create and steer money. This demystifies the world of credit, interest rates and banking that shapes everyday economic life.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering how we become who we are through socialization by family, school, friends and media, and how that process differs by background and gender. They also learn the essentials of political life: what makes political power distinctive, how elections and voting systems work, and how France's institutions are organized. This gives them tools to understand both their own upbringing and the democracy they live in.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.summary: Your teenager is exploring what holds society together and how those bonds can weaken, from family and work ties to new digital forms of connection. They study how norms and social control shape behaviour, why some acts are labelled deviant or criminal, and how people vote, abstain and form public opinion, including the role of polls. It helps them read the social and political currents around them with a critical eye.

clusters.subject: SES
clusters.domain: Utilisation des données quantitatives et des représentations graphiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is picking up the essential number skills for social science: reading and drawing graphs, finding averages and the median, and working with proportions, percentages and rates of change. They also learn to separate a nominal amount from a real one adjusted for prices, so comparisons across time actually make sense. These tools let them handle data confidently from the very start of the course.

clusters.subject: SNT
clusters.summary: Your teenager looks inside the everyday connected gadgets that surround them and learns how a device senses the world, decides what to do, and acts on it. They write small programs to read a sensor or drive a motor, and even build a simple screen or control panel to interact with the object. It demystifies the smart devices they use constantly and shows they can be builders, not just users.

clusters.subject: SNT
clusters.summary: Your teenager discovers how the internet actually moves information around the planet, from the way a web address turns into a machine number to how messages get chopped into packets and routed across many different kinds of networks. They also get a sense of the sheer scale of internet traffic and why some uses of file-sharing are useful while others are illegal. Understanding the plumbing behind the screen helps them make sense of a technology they rely on every day.

clusters.subject: SNT
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns what really happens between pressing the shutter and getting a finished photo, seeing how a camera sensor and hidden calculations build up the image. They peek at the invisible data stored in every picture, like date, camera settings and location, and even write simple code to change an image's colours. It gives them a healthy, informed eye on the photos they take and share all the time.

clusters.subject: SNT
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how web pages are built and how the browser fetches them, from reading and tweaking simple HTML to understanding what a web address and a request actually contain. Just as importantly, they practise browsing safely and thinking critically about search results, online publishing, cookies, and copyright and other rules of the web. These are core digital-citizenship skills for a generation that lives online.

clusters.subject: SNT
clusters.domain: Les données structurées et leur traitement
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns to organise, search, sort and combine data in tables, and to work with real datasets downloaded from open public sources. They also explore how files are stored and synced in the cloud, what personal data is, and the surprising energy cost of the data centres that keep it all running. These skills prepare them to handle information thoughtfully in study, work and daily life.

clusters.subject: SNT
clusters.summary: Your teenager takes an honest look at how social networks work, how they earn money from advertising and personal data, and why the feed shows them what it does. They learn to protect their privacy through account settings, recognise online harassment and where to get help, and understand the legal protections that exist. Given how central these platforms are to teenage life, this helps them use them more safely and knowingly.

clusters.subject: SNT
clusters.summary: Your teenager learns how a phone knows where it is, discovering the satellite signals and calculations behind GPS and how a route gets planned across a map. They explore digital maps, even contributing to a collaborative one, and learn to control whether their location is shared. This gives them real insight into the location and navigation tools they use daily, along with a sense of the privacy choices that come with them.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Adopter un comportement éthique et responsable
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to tell the difference between a personal opinion and knowledge that rests on real evidence, and to let good science guide everyday choices about health and the environment. They also help set the safety rules for lab and field work and start to feel a sense of responsibility for protecting the planet's limited resources.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering how infectious diseases spread, whether directly from person to person or through carriers like mosquitoes, and how an outbreak can turn into an epidemic. By studying real examples such as malaria under the microscope, they learn why hygiene, protective habits and vaccination work, and how a warming climate can push some diseases into new regions.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how cancer begins, when the DNA inside ordinary body cells is damaged over a lifetime by things like tobacco smoke, radiation or certain viruses. Understanding these risk factors helps them see why prevention, screening and vaccines against cancer-linked viruses matter for public health.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Caractéristiques des sols et production de biomasse
clusters.summary: Your teenager is exploring how soil is far more than dirt: it forms slowly from rock combined with living things, and its fertility depends on tiny organisms that recycle dead matter back into nutrients. Through hands-on experiments and species identification, they see why healthy soil is the foundation of the food we grow.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that human sexuality involves both the brain's biology, including its reward and pleasure circuits, and powerful emotional and cultural influences. A key part of the lesson is separating what belongs to private orientation from social roles and expectations, giving them a grounded, respectful understanding of themselves and others.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is studying how animals send and receive signals, through sound, scent, color or display, to feed, defend themselves and find mates. They see how these signals can drive evolution, from a peacock's showy tail to the way communication barriers can eventually split one species into two.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Comportements et stress : vers une vision intégrée de l'organisme
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the body reacts to stress, from the fast surge of adrenaline to the slower cortisol response, and how the nervous, hormonal and immune systems work together as one connected system. They also study what happens when stress becomes chronic and how treatments and calming practices can help the body recover its balance.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering how the brain and nerves turn a sensation into a movement, following the signal from sensory receptors through the spinal cord to the muscles. They study how neurons communicate at synapses, how the brain can rewire itself to recover after injury, and how substances like alcohol and drugs disturb these delicate messages.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Comprendre les conséquences du réchauffement climatique et les possibilités d’actions
clusters.summary: Your teenager is examining the real consequences of a warming planet, on farmland, biodiversity and human health, and the two ways society can respond: reducing emissions and adapting to change. They weigh strategies like carbon storage against real evidence, learn to spot common misconceptions about climate, and see why decades of research now support coordinated action.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Corps humain : de la fécondation à la puberté
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how biological sex is set by chromosomes and genes, and how the reproductive organs develop gradually from before birth all the way through puberty. Using microscopes, documents and diagrams, they build a clear picture of how hormones shape this long process of sexual development.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: D'autres mécanismes contribuent à la diversité du vivant
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering that living things become diverse in ways beyond their genes alone. They learn how learned behaviors like birdsong or human culture pass between generations, and how partnerships with other organisms can extend and reshape an individual's traits.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Des contrastes entre les continents et les océans
clusters.summary: Your teenager is investigating why Earth's surface splits so sharply into high continents and deep ocean floors. By measuring the density of different rocks and reading geological maps, they see that continental crust (mostly granite) and oceanic crust (basalt and gabbro) are genuinely different, and that this difference explains the shape of our planet.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the brain and hormones together control human reproduction, and how this knowledge underpins contraception, protection against sexually transmitted infections, and treatments for infertility. They see how synthetic hormones can mimic natural ones to help people plan whether and when to have children.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the instructions written in DNA are actually read and used by the cell, copied into RNA and then translated into the proteins that build and run the body. They discover that the genetic code is universal to all life, and that a cell can switch genes on or off in response to its needs and its environment.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: L'histoire humaine lue dans son génome
clusters.summary: Your teenager is exploring how our own DNA carries the record of human history, letting scientists trace ancestry and even read the genomes of extinct relatives like Neanderthals from fossils. They learn that traces of these ancient humans survive in people today, and that some of our genes still bear the marks of natural selection, such as the ability to digest milk.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: L'humanité et les écosystèmes : les services écosystémiques et leur gestion
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that ecosystems quietly provide humanity with essentials like food, clean water and pollination, and that we are one part of these systems rather than outside them. Through fieldwork and projects, they see how human activity can disrupt this balance and how scientific knowledge allows us to manage resources more responsibly.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering the body's precise, targeted defenses, the immune cells and antibodies that recognize a specific germ and remember it for next time. They learn how the body generates an almost limitless variety of antibodies, and how this immune memory is exactly what vaccines rely on to protect us.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning about the body's first line of defense, present from birth in all animals, which reacts quickly to injury or infection through inflammation and cells that engulf invaders. Observing inflammation under the microscope, they understand the familiar signs of redness, heat, swelling and pain, and how anti-inflammatory medicines act on them.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: L'inéluctable évolution des génomes au sein des populations
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning why the genetic make-up of populations is always shifting over time, driven by mutation, natural selection and chance (genetic drift). They see how a mathematical model of stable populations helps reveal these forces precisely because real populations never quite match it, and how this can eventually lead to new species.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: L'organisation fonctionnelle des plantes à fleurs
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering how a flowering plant is built to thrive while rooted in one spot, with large surfaces above and below ground to capture light, gases, water and minerals. They study how plants grow and specialize their tissues, how sap moves between roots and leaves, and how hormones and the environment steer development.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: L'organisme pluricellulaire, un ensemble de cellules spécialisées
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that their whole body grew from a single cell, so every cell carries the same DNA yet becomes specialized into different tissues and organs. Working with microscopes, they explore the scales of life from molecules to the whole organism and see how DNA stores the genetic information behind it all.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning why children resemble their parents yet are never identical to them, through the way meiosis and fertilization shuffle and recombine alleles. They apply the classic rules of genetics, including dominant and recessive traits and family pedigrees, to predict how characteristics pass from one generation to the next.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: L'utilisation de l'immunité adaptative en santé humaine
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how medicine puts the immune system to work, from preventive vaccines that train the body against a germ to immunotherapies and monoclonal antibodies used against certain cancers. They also weigh why vaccination needs high coverage to protect a whole community, and how its collective benefit outweighs individual risk.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how landscapes are slowly worn down and reshaped, with water as the main force that weathers rock, carries away the debris and deposits it elsewhere. Through models and simple chemical tests, they connect the type of rock, the climate and the power of a river to how quickly the land changes.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: L'évolution de la biodiversité au cours du temps s'explique par des forces évolutives s'exerçant au niveau des populations
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how the variety of life changes over time through evolution, driven mainly by natural selection and by chance (genetic drift). Using models and real data, they see how populations can split apart and gradually become new species, and they explore why evolution stands as such a well-supported scientific theory.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: La biodiversité change au cours du temps.
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering that the diversity of life is never fixed: it shifts constantly, and Earth's history includes dramatic mass extinctions followed by bursts of new species. By studying fossils and the tree of life, they realize today's species are only a tiny fraction of all that have ever lived, and that humans now influence this change too.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: La caractérisation de la mobilité horizontale
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that Earth's rigid outer shell is broken into plates that slowly move, pulling apart in some places and pushing together in others. Using earthquake maps, GPS measurements and heat-flow data, they see how scientists locate plate boundaries and actually measure how fast the plates travel.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: La complexification des génomes : transferts horizontaux et endosymbioses
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering that genes don't only pass from parent to offspring: organisms can also swap DNA sideways, and whole cells once merged to create the energy-producing parts of our own cells. Through historical experiments, they see how these processes shaped life's genomes and enabled useful biotechnologies today.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how, over thousands of years, humans transformed wild plants into the crops we eat by selecting useful traits, a process that reshaped human diets in return. They compare wild and cultivated plants, explore modern breeding techniques, and consider why protecting crop diversity and seeds matters for feeding the world.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: La dynamique des zones de divergence
clusters.summary: Your teenager is studying how new ocean floor is born where plates pull apart, as rising mantle melts and builds fresh crust at mid-ocean ridges. They learn how this young lithosphere cools, thickens and grows denser as it drifts away, and how seawater seeping through the rocks slowly alters them.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: La plante, productrice de matière organique
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how plants make their own food through photosynthesis, capturing light energy to build organic matter from carbon dioxide and water. Through experiments like pigment chromatography and starch tests, they explore what plants do with that matter, from growth and stored reserves to chemical defenses such as tannins.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: La planète Terre, l'environnement et l'action humaine
clusters.summary: Your child is learning about the Earth as a dynamic planet, from its place in the solar system to the plate movements behind earthquakes and volcanoes, and the winds and currents that drive weather and climate. They begin to understand natural hazards and risk, and how human activity both depends on and affects the environment over immense stretches of geological time.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how a cell copies its entire DNA before dividing, so each new cell receives a faithful duplicate of the genetic information. They explore the classic experiments proving how this copying works and even design a PCR reaction, the same technique used to amplify DNA in labs and medical tests.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Le corps humain et la santé
clusters.summary: Your child is learning how the body works as a coordinated whole, how the heart, lungs and muscles cooperate during effort, how food is digested, and how the nervous system controls it all. They connect this to healthy habits, puberty, responsible choices, and how the body defends itself against germs through hygiene, immunity and vaccination.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Le fonctionnement du système immunitaire humain
clusters.summary: Your teenager is getting an overview of the immune system as an integrated defense against infections, cancer cells and tissue damage. They learn that it combines two strategies, the ancient rapid innate response and the targeted adaptive one, and that our immune abilities keep developing throughout life as we meet new germs and receive vaccines.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that cells run on countless chemical reactions, together called metabolism, that transform matter and energy to meet the cell's needs. Through experiments identifying what reactions consume and produce, they see how living things exchange matter and energy with their surroundings and how a cell's toolkit of enzymes shapes what it can do.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how geologists read time in the rocks, using the order of rock layers and index fossils for relative dating, and radioactive decay to put actual numbers on ages. Combining these methods, they reconstruct the sequence of events that shaped a landscape over millions of years.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your child is building a big-picture understanding of how living things are organized and how they evolve, from how cells are nourished to how species are classified and related. They explore DNA, reproduction and genetic diversity, and gather the evidence that species change over time through evolution and natural selection.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how cells divide, with mitosis producing two identical copies for growth and repair, and meiosis producing reproductive cells with half the chromosomes. By building and reading karyotypes and observing dividing cells under the microscope, they see how chromosomes are duplicated and shared to keep the genetic information intact.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Les enzymes, des biomolécules aux propriétés catalytiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering enzymes, the protein tools that speed up the specific chemical reactions cells depend on to stay alive. They learn how an enzyme's three-dimensional shape lets it recognize just the right molecule, and they measure reaction speeds to see how these biological catalysts actually behave.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how some diseases are caused by a fault in a single gene, how they are passed down through families, and how they show up in the body. Reading family pedigrees, they learn to estimate the risk of a condition appearing in future generations and explore what treatments are possible.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Les traces du passé mouvementé de la Terre
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning to read the clues that record Earth's turbulent past, from ancient mountain belts and rift margins to slivers of old ocean floor trapped inside continents. Using geological maps, they piece together the long cycles of oceans opening and closing and mountains rising and eroding away.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is studying how mountain ranges form when two continents collide and their crust is squeezed, stacked and thickened. Through fieldwork and deep seismic profiles that reveal the crust far below the surface, they learn to spot the tell-tale marks of this immense compression.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning what happens where one plate dives beneath another: the sinking oceanic slab releases water that triggers melting above, feeding explosive volcanoes. They study the special minerals that form under these intense pressures and temperatures and see how the whole process is powered by slow convection deep in the mantle.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that biodiversity exists at several levels, from genetic differences between individuals to the variety of species and ecosystems. Using DNA-comparison software and field surveys, they measure this diversity and grapple with what really defines a species.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Les écosystèmes : des interactions dynamiques entre les êtres vivants et entre eux et leur milieu
clusters.summary: Your teenager is exploring how an ecosystem is a web of living things interacting with each other and their physical surroundings, through competition, predation and cooperation. Through fieldwork, they measure biomass and trace the flows of water, carbon and nitrogen, learning that ecosystems keep a shifting, resilient balance rather than a fixed one.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: L’apport des études sismologiques à la connaissance du globe terrestre
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering how seismic waves act like a scan of the planet, their speed and path revealing the hidden layers of crust, mantle and core. Using physical models and real seismological databases, they learn how scientists map Earth's deep interior without ever going there.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning about the microbiota, the vast community of microbes living in and on the body that outnumbers our own cells and is vital for digestion and immunity. They see how it forms from birth and shifts with diet and antibiotics, why its balance is a marker of health, and how to judge sensible hygiene habits.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Mutations de l'ADN et variabilité génétique
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how mutations, random errors and damage in DNA, arise and how repair systems usually fix them before they stick. They discover that surviving mutations create new versions of genes, fuelling the genetic diversity within a species, with effects that can be harmless, harmful or occasionally beneficial.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your child is building the core habits of scientific thinking: telling facts apart from opinions, reasoning from cause to effect, and using models to make sense of complex things. These skills support responsible citizenship around health and the environment and help them make informed choices, including about their own future path.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning that most diseases stem from many genes and lifestyle factors together, not a single cause, so they must resist oversimplified claims that one gene explains everything. Through basic statistics, mapping and epidemiology, they learn to weigh health data critically and take responsibility for their own health risks.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to work like a scientist: turning curiosity into a clear question, proposing hypotheses, designing experiments and handling tools like the microscope. They practice interpreting results honestly and backing their conclusions with reasoned arguments rather than opinion.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Produire le mouvement : contraction musculaire et apport d'énergie
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how muscles produce movement and where they get the energy to do it, from the actin-myosin machinery inside muscle cells to the ATP supplied by respiration and fermentation. They connect this to how the body regulates blood sugar, what goes wrong in diabetes, and the health effects of doping substances.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Reconstituer et comprendre les variations climatiques passées
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how scientists reconstruct climates from millions of years ago, using clues in ice, ocean sediments and rocks to read past temperatures. They connect these ancient swings to the carbon cycle, plate tectonics and Earth's orbit, giving them the context to understand today's human-driven warming.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Reproduction de la plante entre vie fixée et mobilité
clusters.summary: Your teenager is discovering how plants, rooted in one place, still manage to reproduce by enlisting wind and animals to carry their pollen and disperse their seeds. Dissecting flowers, they link structure to function and explore the long partnership, or coevolution, between plants and their pollinators.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Se situer dans l'espace et dans le temps
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to picture the vast time scales of Earth and life, from the planet's age to the long story of evolution, and to place the human species within the tree of life. They also see how one phenomenon can be understood at different scales, and that scientific knowledge is built and revised over time.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how farmland, or agrosystems, is managed to produce the biomass humanity needs for food and materials, and how a farmer's choices and the local land shape it. Through farm data and visits, they see why crops need added inputs to keep producing when so much biomass is harvested and taken away.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how loose sediments turn into solid rock through burial, compaction and cementing, and how different rocks record the environments where they formed. By studying these rocks under the microscope, they learn to reconstruct what an ancient landscape looked like.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Variation génétique bactérienne et résistance aux antibiotiques
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning why antibiotics are losing their power, as random mutations make some bacteria resistant and antibiotic use then lets those survivors multiply. Using evolutionary reasoning and lab protocols like the antibiogram, they understand why overuse worsens the problem and why responsible use is a major public-health concern.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.domain: Vers une gestion durable des agrosystèmes
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how farming affects soil and the surrounding environment, and how the impact depends on the model of agriculture chosen. They take a scientific approach to real sustainability problems, exploring how research is producing knowledge and practices for farming that can feed people while protecting the land.

clusters.subject: SVT
clusters.summary: Your teenager is learning how erosion connects to everyday life, supplying the sand and stone in materials we use, while also creating risks like landslides and coastal loss. Using databases and images, they measure how fast erosion works today and see how human activity can either worsen or limit it.

clusters.subject: Sciences de l'ingénieur
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to take a real engineered product apart in their mind and figure out how it actually works: what forces and energy flow through it, how well it performs versus what was expected, and how its electronics, software and networks fit together. They also learn to reason carefully about measurements, comparing real results with predictions and asking whether the numbers can be trusted. This is the heart of thinking like an engineer, and it prepares them to diagnose and improve the technology they will meet in higher studies and work.

clusters.subject: Sciences de l'ingénieur
clusters.summary: Your child is learning to explain technical ideas clearly to different audiences, choosing what matters and presenting it convincingly out loud and in writing.
